name: generate
context: Generate typed SDKs, API clients, server stubs, models, and docs assets from a validated OpenAPI contract.
info: OpenAPI generation turns an API description into code artifacts such as TypeScript clients, Python clients, Java clients, Go clients, server skeletons, models, API docs, and test fixtures. The primary execution surface is OpenAPI Generator CLI, available through npm, Homebrew, Docker, PyPI, or JAR distribution. Agents use this skill when integrating with an API, publishing SDKs, creating server stubs from contract-first designs, or regenerating code after a spec update. Generation quality depends on the contract: stable `operationId`, clear schemas, examples, discriminators, security schemes, and validation before generation.
triggers: create a typescript sdk from swagger
body:
# OpenAPI Code Generation

Generate code only after [[openapi/validate]] passes. Generation converts contract decisions into long-lived source code, so small spec problems become client API problems. Stable `operationId` values, reusable schemas, discriminators, and explicit auth are more important than generator flags.

## Generate a Client

```bash
# Default wrapper: TypeScript fetch client
nodes/openapi/scripts/generate-openapi.sh openapi.yaml

# Explicit generator and output directory
nodes/openapi/scripts/generate-openapi.sh openapi.yaml typescript-fetch generated/api-client

# Add generator-specific properties
OPENAPI_GENERATOR_ADDITIONAL_PROPERTIES=npmName=@acme/api-client,supportsES6=true \
  nodes/openapi/scripts/generate-openapi.sh openapi.yaml typescript-fetch generated/api-client
```

## Common Generators

```bash
# TypeScript
nodes/openapi/scripts/generate-openapi.sh openapi.yaml typescript-fetch generated/ts-fetch
nodes/openapi/scripts/generate-openapi.sh openapi.yaml typescript-axios generated/ts-axios

# Python
nodes/openapi/scripts/generate-openapi.sh openapi.yaml python generated/python-client

# Go
nodes/openapi/scripts/generate-openapi.sh openapi.yaml go generated/go-client

# Server stubs
nodes/openapi/scripts/generate-openapi.sh openapi.yaml nodejs-express-server generated/server
nodes/openapi/scripts/generate-openapi.sh openapi.yaml spring generated/spring-server
```

## Inspect Options

```bash
npx @openapitools/openapi-generator-cli list
npx @openapitools/openapi-generator-cli config-help -g typescript-fetch
npx @openapitools/openapi-generator-cli generate -i openapi.yaml -g typescript-fetch -o generated/client --skip-validate-spec
```

Avoid `--skip-validate-spec` unless debugging a generator issue. In normal work, validation failures should stop generation.

## Generated Code Policy

Decide whether generated code is committed. Commit generated clients when downstream users consume them directly from the repository or when releases package the output. Do not commit generated server stubs as application code unless the project already owns them. In CI, regenerate and compare output when generated artifacts are committed.

sections.title: Generation workflow
sections.body: Validate and bundle the spec first with [[openapi/validate]]. Choose the generator target, inspect its config with `config-help`, set package names and output options, generate into a clean output directory, then run the generated package's own tests or build. Never patch generated code manually unless the project has no better extension point.

sections.title: Generator choice
sections.body: Pick generators by consumer ecosystem: `typescript-fetch` for small browser or Node clients with fetch, `typescript-axios` for projects standardized on axios, `python` for Python SDKs, `go` for Go clients, and server generators only for contract-first scaffolding. Inspect generated output before committing to a generator.

sections.title: Contract quality for generators
sections.body: Generated SDKs are only as usable as the spec. Use unique operation IDs, simple schema names, explicit required fields, discriminators for polymorphism, clear error models, and reusable security schemes. Avoid anonymous inline objects for important request and response bodies because generated type names can become unstable.

sections.title: CI regeneration
sections.body: When generated code is committed, CI should validate the spec, regenerate into the committed output directory, and fail if `git diff --exit-code` reports drift. This guarantees the client package matches the contract in the same commit.

links.context: Author operation IDs and schemas that generate clean code
Use generated TypeScript clients in frontend or Node projects
Use generated Python clients or Schemathesis tooling
Run the npm OpenAPI Generator wrapper

related.note: Generation should consume a valid, bundled contract
TypeScript is a common generated client target
Some TypeScript projects prefer deriving runtime schemas from OpenAPI or vice versa

scripts.name: Generate From OpenAPI
scripts.description: Run OpenAPI Generator CLI against a spec with configurable generator, output directory, and additional properties

OPENAPI_GENERATOR_VERSION.description: Generator version selected by the npm wrapper when supported

JAVA_HOME.description: Java runtime location for the generator JAR

generator.description: OpenAPI Generator target such as typescript-fetch

depends.reason: Generation should use a validated contract
The wrapper uses npx when openapi-generator-cli is not installed

frontmatter.author: dojo-community
frontmatter.audience: SDK maintainers, backend engineers, frontend engineers, AI agents
